description: Challenge System — progressive difficulty challenges across all Cloud Engineering domains. Challenges are designed to be solved independently, not as guided labs.

debugging.description: Broken system — diagnose and fix. Root cause analysis required.

architecture.description: Design a system given constraints. Defend your choices.

optimization.description: Improve an existing system — performance, cost, or security.

scenario.description: Real-world production scenario. Make decisions under pressure.

build.description: Build something from scratch with specific requirements.

challenges.title: The Vanishing Disk Space
challenges.scenario: A developer at CloudNova reports their application server is 'out of disk space.' You SSH in and `df -h` shows 65% used. Yet applications fail with 'No space left on device.' What's really happening? Diagnose and fix.
hints: Check inode usage, not just disk space
Look for directories with millions of tiny files
success_criteria: Finds and resolves the file accumulation

challenges.title: Design the CloudNova VNet
challenges.scenario: CloudNova needs a production VNet for a new microservice platform. Requirements: 50+ services, 3 tiers (web, app, data), must isolate public-facing from internal services, support future growth to 200+ services, and integrate with on-premises via VPN.
success_criteria: Subnet plan accounts for all 3 tiers

challenges.title: The Leaking S3 Bucket
challenges.scenario: Security alerts: a CloudNova storage bucket containing customer PII is publicly accessible. 847 objects exposed. You discover this at 2 PM on a Friday. What do you do? Walk through your incident response — immediate action, investigation, remediation, and prevention.
hints: Then investigate: how did this happen?
Finally prevent: ensure it can't happen again
success_criteria: Root cause identified (misconfigured policy, missing guardrail)
Prevention: bucket policies, SCP, or automated scanning implemented

challenges.title: Build the CloudNova DevOps Pipeline
challenges.scenario: CloudNova's infrastructure team manually deploys to production by running Terraform from laptops. This has caused 3 production incidents this quarter. Design and justify a CI/CD pipeline for Terraform that: prevents manual apply, requires PR review, runs plan on PR, applies on merge, and includes cost estimation and security scanning.
hints: GitHub Actions + Terraform Cloud or OIDC
Add guardrails: cost estimation, security scan, approval gates
success_criteria: Describes how manual apply is prevented

challenges.title: Kubernetes Cluster Meltdown
challenges.scenario: It's 3:17 AM. PagerDuty wakes you: 'AKS node pool at 100% memory, pods OOMKilled, API returning 503s.' The new ML inference service deployed 4 hours ago. You're the on-call engineer. Walk through your incident response — from first alert to resolution.
hints: Consider cordoning nodes, scaling, or rolling back
success_criteria: Root cause: ML service resource misconfiguration
Prevention: resource requests/limits, HPA, monitoring alerts

challenges.title: Cost Optimization Treasure Hunt
challenges.scenario: CloudNova's monthly Azure bill is $47,000. The CFO wants it under $35,000 by next quarter — without degrading service. You're given: resource inventory, 3 months of Cost Management data, and read-only access. Find $12,000/month in savings. Document your findings and recommendations.
success_criteria: Each recommendation has cost impact quantified
No recommendations degrade performance or availability

challenges.title: Architect the CloudNova AI Platform
challenges.scenario: CloudNova wants to deploy an internal AI platform. Requirements: serve 5 LLM models, vector search for 10M+ documents, RAG pipeline, GPU scheduling, model versioning, cost tracking per team, and self-service for data scientists. Budget: $25K/month. Design the architecture.
hints: Vector DB: pgvector vs Qdrant tradeoffs

challenges.title: The APT Incident at CloudNova
challenges.scenario: A nation-state APT has been detected in CloudNova's environment. They've been present for 3 weeks, exfiltrated data from 2 databases, and left backdoors in 4 services. You're the incident commander. Walk through the full incident response: containment, eradication, recovery, post-incident. Coordinate: Security, Infrastructure, Legal, PR, and Executive teams.

challenges.title: Build the Capstone Platform
challenges.scenario: Integrate all previous projects into one production platform. One `terraform apply` provisions everything. One GitOps repo manages everything. One observability stack monitors it all. Include: landing zone, AKS cluster, GitOps pipeline, observability stack, AI inference endpoint. Document with ADRs and a polished case study.
hints: Start from your Project 004 landing zone

challenges.title: Production Database Migration (Zero Downtime)
challenges.scenario: CloudNova's primary PostgreSQL database (500GB, 5000 TPS) needs to migrate from VMs to a managed service. Acceptable downtime: zero. You have a 4-hour maintenance window where degraded performance is acceptable but complete downtime is not. Design and execute the migration plan.
